method.compaction_definition: Consecutive proxy-K entries where total drops > 500 tokens
method.work_definition: W = tokens_before - tokens_after (tokens removed per compaction event)
method.temperature_definition: T = mean per-session growth rate from positive-delta proxy-K entries = 548.3 t/session (excludes anomalous session=0 entry; original S381 included it and got T=567.8)
method.session_range: S74..S402 (198 unique sessions in git history)
method.new_data_vs_s381: 4 new proxy-K entries (S383, S384, S399, S402). S383->S384 is one new compaction event (W=846t). 3 hidden events estimated from commit messages (S306, S370, S377).
tests: Temporal split (early S<=200 vs late S>200)

compaction_events_hidden.source: commit messages: FRONTIER archival + PRINCIPLES trim + PHILOSOPHY trim, drift 11.6%->8.4%
commit cbc1a427: PRINCIPLES 800t + FRONTIER 500t + DEPS 500t explicitly stated in commit body

extended_analysis_n12.note: Includes 3 hidden events estimated from commit messages

crooks_test.verdict: Cannot test full Crooks theorem -- no reverse (spontaneous growth-exceeding) events observable. Forward work distribution consistent with irreversible Crooks regime: exp(-W/T) spans 3.4 orders of magnitude across the 9 measured events.

temporal_split.efficiency_trend: DECREASING (early 73.1% -> late 54.0% at n=9; 73.1% -> 67.6% at n=12). Late compaction events remove more absolute tokens (higher mean W in n=9 late split) but are proportionally smaller (lower pct_drop: 4.3% vs 7.4%). The system has grown 2.5x (25kt to 63kt) so same absolute W represents smaller fractional work.

threshold_sensitivity.note: J is threshold-sensitive: ranging from 0.044 to 0.180 across thresholds. All CIs exclude 1.0.

fractional_jarzynski.note: Fractional view (W_rel = W/K_before) normalizes for system growth. Mean compaction removes 6.3% of tokens. Fractional efficiency 61.3% is more stable than absolute (65.8%).

comparison_to_s381.summary: Core findings RECONFIRMED with extended data. J shifts from 0.097 to 0.063, moving FURTHER from 1.0 (stronger rejection of Jarzynski near-equilibrium). CI narrows. Efficiency improves from 59.9% to 65.8%. Same 9 measured events; 3 hidden events discovered in commit history.

n_events.change: Same measured events; +3 hidden events discovered via commit message analysis

J.note: Primarily due to T change (567.8 vs 548.3) from excluding anomalous session=0 entry and adding 3 new growth intervals with lower rates

CI_95.note: CI tightens; upper bound drops from 0.184 to 0.110

T.note: S381 included session=0 in growth rate calculation; S403 excludes it as anomalous

kurtosis.note: Kurtosis excess, not raw. S381 may have used a different convention (raw kurtosis 2.399 vs excess 0.193). At n=9, kurtosis is highly unstable.

comparison_to_s381.verdict_change: PARTIALLY CONFIRMED -> RECONFIRMED (Crooks regime). Jarzynski equality exclusion STRENGTHENED (J moves further from 1.0, CI narrows). New finding: proxy-K log sparseness masks ~25% of compaction events.

P1_J_excludes_1.prediction: J should still exclude 1.0 (Crooks regime confirmed)
P1_J_excludes_1.verdict: CONFIRMED -- J=0.063, CI [0.021, 0.110] excludes 1.0 even more strongly than S381

P2_n_increases_to_12_plus.prediction: N should increase from 9 to 12+ events
P2_n_increases_to_12_plus.verdict: PARTIALLY CONFIRMED -- n=9 measured (same events), but 3 hidden compaction events discovered in commit history (S306, S370, S377) bringing total to 12. Proxy-K log sampling rate (29% of sessions) is the bottleneck, not compaction frequency.
P2_n_increases_to_12_plus.implication: Proxy-K log needs denser sampling to capture all compaction events. Current ~1-in-3 sessions measured.

P3_skewness_increases.prediction: Work distribution skewness should increase with scale
P3_skewness_increases.verdict: CONFIRMED -- skewness 0.867 -> 1.164 (n=9) or 1.485 (n=12). Right-skew increases as the dominant S126->S127 event (W=5072t, 18.8% drop) becomes increasingly anomalous relative to the shrinking typical compaction size.

P4_efficiency_improves.prediction: Efficiency should improve over time (compaction gets more targeted)
P4_efficiency_improves.verdict: FALSIFIED -- efficiency DECREASES over time (73.1% early -> 54.0% late at n=9, 67.6% late at n=12). Late compaction events have lower pct_drop (4.3% vs 7.4%) suggesting more conservative/smaller interventions, but this reduces thermodynamic efficiency. The system's larger size makes each compaction proportionally smaller and more dissipative.
P4_efficiency_improves.mechanism: As the system grows from ~25kt to ~65kt, compaction targets shift from core files (T3 knowledge) to sprawling T4 tools (maintenance.py alone = 27kt). Larger, more heterogeneous systems have higher compaction overhead per useful token removed.

proxy_k_log_undersampling.finding: The proxy-K log captures only 29% of sessions. At least 3 compaction events (S306, S370, S377) totaling ~4820t of work are invisible to consecutive-drop analysis because growth between sparse measurements masks the drops.
proxy_k_log_undersampling.implication: True compaction frequency is ~1 event per 17 sessions (12/198), not 1 per 22 (9/198). True total compaction work is ~25.6kt (12 events), not 20.8kt (9 events). The Jarzynski estimator with n=12 gives J=0.065 vs J=0.063 at n=9 -- hidden events do not materially change the conclusion.
proxy_k_log_undersampling.recommendation: Proxy-K measurements should be taken every session (not every ~3 sessions) to capture all compaction events.

j_moves_further_from_1.finding: J decreased from 0.097 (S381) to 0.063 (S403), a 37% drop. The system is moving FURTHER from Jarzynski equilibrium, not converging toward it.
j_moves_further_from_1.mechanism: Primarily driven by T reduction (567.8 -> 548.3, from excluding session=0 and adding lower-growth-rate recent intervals). The growth rate is slowing as the system matures, while compaction work size remains roughly constant -- increasing W/T and driving J toward 0.
j_moves_further_from_1.implication: The thermodynamic analogy predicts: as growth slows but compaction events remain similar in size, the system becomes increasingly irreversible. This is consistent with the knowledge system maturing (less explosive growth, same maintenance burden).

bimodal_compaction_character.finding: Compaction events cluster into two types: (1) surgical trims (W < 2000t, pct_drop 1-6%, 8 of 9 events) and (2) structural overhauls (W > 4000t, pct_drop 7-19%, 2 of 9 events). The S126->S127 event (5072t, 18.8%) and S335->S339 event (4308t, 7.2%) are qualitatively different from the others.
bimodal_compaction_character.implication: The work distribution is not a single process but a mixture. Surgical trims are maintenance; structural overhauls are phase transitions. This bimodality is why the cumulant expansion fails (delta=2.20) -- it assumes unimodal Gaussian-like work distribution.

efficiency_paradox.finding: Efficiency DECREASES as the system matures (73.1% early -> 54.0% late at n=9). This contradicts the prediction that compaction would become more targeted.
efficiency_paradox.mechanism: Early compaction (S75-S171) operated on a ~25kt system with concentrated knowledge files. Late compaction (S335-S384) operates on a ~60kt system dominated by maintenance.py (27kt alone = 40% of total proxy-K). Compacting a heterogeneous, tool-heavy system is inherently less efficient than compacting a concentrated knowledge system.
efficiency_paradox.implication: The dissipation floor is not constant -- it grows with system complexity. The 40% dissipation from L-730 may be an era-specific value, not a structural minimum.

conclusion: F-SP6 Jarzynski equality hardening at N=779 lessons. Core result RECONFIRMED and STRENGTHENED: J=0.063 (CI [0.021, 0.110], further from 1.0 than S381's J=0.097). Crooks far-from-equilibrium regime persists. Second law holds (<W>=2307t >= dF=1518t, efficiency 65.8%). 4 predictions tested: 2 CONFIRMED (J excludes 1.0; skewness increases), 1 PARTIALLY CONFIRMED (n increases to 12 but 3 are hidden in commit history), 1 FALSIFIED (efficiency decreases over time, not improves). New findings: (1) proxy-K log undersampling masks ~25% of compaction events, (2) J moving further from 1.0 as growth rate slows, (3) bimodal compaction character (surgical trims vs structural overhauls), (4) efficiency paradox -- system maturity increases thermodynamic irreversibility.
